working_title: Prayer to Saint Ambrose of Optina | Text and Icon

preferred_prayer.title: Original Prayer to Saint Ambrose of Optina
preferred_prayer.rights_note: Original prayer composed from historical facts and teachings (Option B). Cleared for on-screen text and audio recitation.

fallback_prayers.title: Akathist to the Most Holy Theotokos
fallback_prayers.use_case: Fallback if no rights-safe Ambrose Akathist text can be used.

voice_plan.preferred: permissioned real Orthodox priest, monk, deacon, chanter, or reader recording
voice_plan.not_allowed: unauthorized cloning or imitation of a real priest or monk

visual_plan.primary: verified icon of Saint Ambrose of Optina with readable prayer text overlay
visual_plan.fallback: reverent icon-style still plus source/rights-safe text cards
visual_plan.notes: Keep the icon visible; text must be large enough to read on mobile.

opening_context_draft: People often turn to Saint Ambrose of Optina in times of grief, chronic illness, discouragement, and when seeking humble spiritual guidance. This prayer is offered as a companion to his life story, not as a promise of a specific outcome, but as a way to pray with the Church for mercy, comfort, and peace.

working_title: Akathist of Repentance | Prayer With Text and Icon

preferred_prayer.title: Akathist of Repentance
preferred_prayer.rights_note: Public domain English translation sourced from Podvorye of Novospassky Monastery in Sumarokovo. Cleared for on-screen text and audio recitation.

fallback_prayers.title: Akathist to the Most Holy Theotokos
fallback_prayers.use_case: Fallback if no rights-safe Repentance Akathist text can be used.

voice_plan.preferred: permissioned real Orthodox priest, monk, deacon, chanter, or reader recording
voice_plan.not_allowed: unauthorized cloning or imitation of a real priest or monk

visual_plan.primary: verified portrait of Hegumen Nikon Vorobiev with readable prayer text overlay
visual_plan.fallback: reverent icon-style still plus source/rights-safe text cards
visual_plan.notes: Keep the portrait visible; text must be large enough to read on mobile.

opening_context_draft: Hegumen Nikon Vorobiev was a modern elder and teacher of repentance. Since he is not formally canonized, there is no official Akathist hymn dedicated to him. In keeping with his teachings, this prayer companion presents the Akathist of Repentance. It is offered as a way to pray for mercy, sobriety, humility, and the recovery of truth before God.
